articles.full_text:
Red alert for heavy rain, thunderstorm for next 24 hours in 10 states, what is weather forecast for Delhi, Noida, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar?
Several Indian states are under red alert as IMD warns of heavy rain, thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds over the next 24 hours.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a red alert for heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds in several states over the next 24 hours. While some regions may witness intense rainfall and lightning, others are expected to face rising temperatures and humid conditions. Delhi-NCR, Uttar Pradesh and Bihar remain under close watch as weather activity increases across north and east India.
IMD issues red alert in 10 states
IMD has warned of heavy rainfall, thunderstorms, and strong winds in multiple states over the next 24 hours. Wind speeds may reach up to 70 km/h in some areas.
Delhi-NCR may witness sudden weather change
Delhi, Noida and Gurugram are likely to experience cloudy skies, gusty winds and possible evening thunderstorms. Temperatures may remain high during the daytime.
Uttar Pradesh on storm and heat alert
Several cities in Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow, Kanpur and Varanasi, may witness hot weather along with thunderstorms and strong winds in some districts.
Bihar braces for Rain and Dusty winds
Patna, Gaya and nearby areas may receive light to moderate rainfall along with dusty winds and thunderstorms over the next 24 hours.
Rajasthan continues to battle extreme heat
Rajasthan is expected to remain under intense heatwave conditions, although a few districts may receive light rain and strong winds for brief relief.
Madhya Pradesh faces mixed weather
Some parts of Madhya Pradesh may witness severe heat, while other regions could experience rain, lightning and even hailstorm activity.
Tamil Nadu and South India likely to get heavy rain
Due to a low-pressure system over the Bay of Bengal, parts of Tamil Nadu and nearby southern states may witness heavy rainfall and thunderstorms.
Public advisory
The weather department has advised people to avoid unnecessary travel during storms, stay hydrated during heatwave conditions and remain indoors during lightning activity.
